title: acer tataricum var. ginnala
intro: Acer tataricum var. ginnala, also known as Amur sweet spire or Schwer's ginnala, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to East Asia, characterized by its fragrant, white flowers and edible fruit. The plant's bark, leaves, and roots have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Acer tataricum var. ginnala include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, are made from its leaves, bark, and roots, and are used to harness its medicinal properties.
